summary: Safety-limitations review accepts the candidate because the proposed change is provenance-bounded: it creates metadata-only PubMed EFetch source snapshots and links source_snapshot_id provenance without adding new treatment recommendations, efficacy claims, or safety generalizations. The named senolytics records retain pilot-study, small-sample, abstract-extraction, and adverse-event caveats.

findings.claim_or_issue: The candidate links PubMed EFetch source_snapshot_id values and creates source-snapshot metadata for PMIDs 31542391, 36857968, and 40010154.
findings.why_it_matters: A provenance-only repair should not make dasatinib plus quercetin appear safer or more effective than the underlying early human evidence supports.
findings.recommended_action: No change required; keep the candidate bounded to source-snapshot provenance and do not treat this repair as a certainty upgrade.

findings.claim_or_issue: The reviewed finding, result, study, and risk-of-bias records describe small open-label or pilot randomized studies and include limits such as low confidence, exploratory analysis, non-definitive efficacy, single-arm design, and adverse-event boundaries.
findings.why_it_matters: Senolytic human evidence here involves small disease-specific studies, so missing caveats could overstate clinical readiness or general longevity relevance.
findings.recommended_action: No change required for this lane; preserve the existing caveats when later extraction or synthesis reviews update these records.

findings.claim_or_issue: The three source snapshots set access_tier to metadata_only, mark raw_storage.stored as false, and state that PubMed EFetch is not a reusable full-text source by itself.
findings.why_it_matters: The snapshot records should not be mistaken for full-text evidence capable of resolving detailed safety-event tables or article-level limitations.
findings.recommended_action: No change required; use separate full-text or registry artifacts before drawing stronger adverse-event or safety conclusions.
